name: ruby-thor
title: ruby-thor
description:
Ruby scripting framework
Thor is a simple and efficient scripting framework for building
self-documenting command line utilities.
.
It can be used to replace rake, sake and rubigen.

commands.name: thor
commands.description: Scripting framework for command line utilities

arguments.description: this program, for the Debian GNU/Linux system (but may be freely used by others).
arguments.raw: -line  this program, for the Debian GNU/Linux system (but may be freely used by others).

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use thor because Ruby scripting framework.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use thor as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `thor help [TASK]` as documented upstream for thor.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `thor install NAME` as documented upstream for thor.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `thor installed` as documented upstream for thor.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `thor list [SEARCHTERM]` as documented upstream for thor.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair thor with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `thor --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -line
arguments.description: this program, for the Debian GNU/Linux system (but may be freely used by others).

documentation.readme: thor - Scripting framework for command line utilities
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Ruby scripting framework.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: thor - Scripting framework for command line utilities.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install ruby-thor
Install with: sudo apt install ruby-thor
